title: nymphoides parviflora
intro: Nymphoides parviflora, also known as the small-flowered water-lily or small yellow water-lily, is a species of aquatic plant in the family Menyanthaceae. It is a perennial herb native to East Asia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. It can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create herbal remedies for various health conditions.
